title: Debugging, Review, and Adversarial Prompting · Evaluation Brief: Cause Repair
summary: Diagnose a failing build and review the repair against a base branch. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt cause repair increment in an evidence-first debug and review playbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prompt for the smallest cause-level repair and a regression test.
Rank competing hypotheses and choose discriminating checks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Cause Repair
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt debug review. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt debug review. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cause Repair contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for prompt for the smallest cause-level repair and a regression test.

content.text: Prompt for the smallest cause-level repair and a regression test. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt debug review. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Cause Repair, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an evidence-first debug and review playbook.

content.title: Non-worked case

steps.title: Build Cause Repair evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-cause-repair-1q32r6" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Cause Repair into observable performance evidence.

Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]

steps.title: Defend the Cause Repair model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Cause Repair applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prompt for the smallest cause-level repair and a regression test.
